ocr.text: In the ever-evolving landscape of the banking sector, the performance of branch networks remains a critical focus for
institutions striving to enhance customer engagement and operational efficiency. Deutsche Bank, as a pivotal player in the
North American banking and commercial finance arena, continuously assesses its branch productivity and customer retention
strategies to ensure alignment with market demands and competitive pressures. The accompanying data table illustrates key
performance metrics that reflect our branch network's effectiveness and the broader implications for our strategic initiatives.
The Branch Productivity metric serves as a cornerstone for evaluating the efficiency of individual branches within our network,
capturing the output relative to the input resources allocated.
BranchCount  AssetsUnderManagement  CustomerSatisfactionIndex
OperatingMargin  ReturnonEquity  CosttoIncomeRatio
LoanGrowthRate  DepositsGrowthRate  NetInterestMargin
BranchProductivity  CustomerRetentionRate  MarketShare
This measure is essential for identifying high-performing branches that can serve as models for best practices, as well as
underperforming locations that may require targeted interventions or resource reallocation. In conjunction with this, the
Customer Retention Rate provides insight into our ability to maintain long-term relationships with clients, a critical aspect of
sustaining revenue and market presence. High retention rates not only indicate customer satisfaction but also reflect the success

of our engagement strategies and the quality of service provided across our branches.

Market dynamics in North America are
characterized by increased competition among financial institutions and the growing significance of digital channels. As
customers increasingly demand seamless multi-channel experiences, understanding branch performance metrics becomes vital
for informing our strategic decisions. The interplay between branch productivity and customer retention can offer valuable
insights into the effectiveness of our current operational strategies and help guide future investments in both physical and digital

infrastructures.

Moreover, the data presented highlights regional variations and trends that may influence our market share. By
closely examining the performance of our branches, we can identify opportunities for expansion or consolidation, ultimately
allowing us to respond proactively to shifts in consumer behavior and competitive landscapes. This data-driven approach
ensures that Deutsche Bank remains agile and responsive, ready to adapt our business model to meet the evolving needs of our

clientele while maximizing our operational efficiency.

In summary, the forthcoming data table on branch network performance
serves as a critical tool for assessing our current standing in the marketplace. By focusing on metrics such as Branch
Productivity and Customer Retention Rate, we position ourselves to make informed decisions that enhance our strategic
direction and support sustainable growth in the North American banking sector.
